ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Dec-14-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Beverly Lavin for and with Farid Bagheroghli.

The Court asked if there was anything further regarding the request for attorney's fees. Ms. Lavin stated that there is no estate in the conservatorship case, and that the examiner suggested going back to Contra Costa County for the fees. The Court stated that they should have obtained an order from Contra Costa County regarding the fees and asked about venue. Ms. Lavin stated that Wells Fargo is a national bank including offices in San Francisco. The Court stated that Contra Costa County is also an option for venue, that the trustees are not allowed to decide and that venue must be where the trustee resides or their usual place of business. The Court granted the accounting petition (subject to review of the order and review of the attorney fees). A status for filing a petition for change of venue is set for January 25, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Paul H. Alvarado. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Henry. (Not Reported)

Petition For Increase In Monthly Payment To Minor'S Parents
Off Calendar May-13-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Beverly Lavin for and with Farid Bagheroghli.

The Court stated that the issue before the Court is what is an appropriate compensation to the Family for the care they provide for Savannah to live in the home, expressed concern about all of the refinances on the home resulting in a large mortgage, stated that it appears there is no more money to be taken out on the home and that it was prepared to grant an increase but not for the amount requested. Ms. Lavin and Mr. Bagheroghli presented oral argument to support their positions, explained that they applied for Medi Cal several times but that they were turned down because of the amount of money in Savannah's account and that they have never been able to obtain any help from the County with regard to respid care. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Petition For Increase In Monthly Payment To Minor'S Parents
Off Calendar Apr-26-2011 Continued To May-10-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #10, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Feb-04-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Beverly Lavin for Wells Fargo Bank and Farid Bagheroghli.

The Court stated that a further declaration was filed describing the investment but not the risk. In addition, the Court expressed concern about the Bank investing in their own product, stated that it would not order diversion of the funds and that there is to be no further investments into Hedge funds. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #10, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Dec-21-2010 Continued To Jan-26-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Beverly Lavin for and with Farid Bagheroghli.

The Court stated that the matter was on for the 10th Account and Report, that the Examiner had questions, and noted that the Trustee fees are calculated at the end of the time period not monthly. Ms. Lavin stated that she thinks Wells Fargo is aware of that information. The Court then addressed the complimentary strategies and the Hedge fund. Ms. Lavin stated that they are trying to diversify. The Court stated that it had concerns regarding there attempts to diversify. Ms. Lavin stated that Farid was aware that they were taking their own management fees and that they were in another fund at the beginning of the account period but not now. Mr. Bagheroghli stated that there was a loss on that one. Ms. Lavin stated that the Trust has continued to increase. Mr. Bagheroghli stated that they were the only bank where the money has increased and that Wells Fargo has done a decent job. Ms. Lavin stated that she asked Wells Fargo to respond. The Court stated that it is concerned, that Wells Fargo has not responded, that it is not ok just to say that they were diversifying, and that it is also concerned with the separate management fee. Additional discussions regarding Wells Fargo were held. The Court then addressed the gifts stating that it is not inclined to approve the gift to the parents. Mr. Bagheroghli argued in support of the gift. The Court stated that it didn't think it was appropriate to gift from a Special Needs Trust. Ms. Lavin stated that it is not a Special Needs Trust. The Court stated that there could be an approach if the monthly amount to the parents isn't enough. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Gordon Aiavao, CSR#11216. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: none present.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #10, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Nov-08-2010 Continued To Nov-24-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Beverly Lavin for and with Farid Bagheroghli.

Ms. Lavin stated that a declaration addressing the issues raised by the Probate Examiner was filed on October 15, 2010, explained the length of time it took to get a response from Wells Fargo and stated that she would file the declarations from the Co-Trustees (Wells Fargo and Mr. Bagheroghli). The accounting was taken under submission for the Court's review of all three declarations.

Judge: Mary Wiss. Reporter: Robert Balian, CSR#5220. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING , REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Apr-28-2008 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Beverly Lavin for and with Farid Bagheroghli (Trustee).

The Court stated that the issue regarding the estate planning was premature and questioned the amount of fees being charged. The matter was taken under submission. The Court will sign an order allowing the accounting to be done every two years, which is to be submitted by Ms. Lavin.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy F. Smith.
